MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Plaintiffs Based On The Underlying Hoa Action And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Victoria Yeager
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VICTORIA YEAGER, Tel No. (530) 432-7063 appearing in pro per;
Atty. CLAUDIA L. STONE of Nemecek & Cole, Tel No. (818) 788-9500 appearing for Defendant De La Pena & McDonald, LLP, Charles Murray, and Steven E. McDonald.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP, CHARLES MURRAY, AND STEVEN E. MCDONALD (SUED AS STEPHEN E. MCDONALD) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Plaintiffs Based On The Underlying HOA Action And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Victoria Yeager, the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the Fifth Cause of Action for Professional Negligence in the HOA matter is granted. Defendants meet their initial burden by presenting evidence that Defendants withdrew as counsel three years before the trial in the case began, Plaintiffs were subsequently represented by new counsel, Plaintiffs had judgment entered against them because the Court found they were unjustly enriched and not because of any identifiable mistake of Defendants, and Plaintiffs' trial counsel was responsible for presenting evidence in support of the cross-complaint. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 22, 25, 27, 34-35, 38-43, 46-48, 55.) Plaintiffs' evidence does not raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiffs would have achieved a better result at trial, but for Defendants' conduct. The Declaration of Victoria Yeager speculates that had Defendants done certain things differently, the trial court would have come to a different conclusion. "Responsive evidence that gives rise to no more than mere speculation is not sufficient to establish a triable issue of material fact." (Carlsen v. Koivumaki (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 879, 889-890.) Motion for Summary Judgment as to Plaintiff Victoria Yeager is denied. Defendants meet their initial burden by providing evidence showing that Defendants only represented Plaintiffs in five cases. (Declaration of Steven McDonald, par. 4.) The Declaration of Victoria Yeager raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants represented Plaintiffs in more than five matters. (Declaration of Victoria Yeager, par. 65.) The First Amended Complaint is broadly written and does not limit the cause of action to only the five cases identified by Defendants. (First Amended Complaint, par. 28.) The evidentiary objections will be addressed in a separate order. ORDER AFTER HEARING AND ORDER ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP, STEPHEN MCDONALD and CHARLES MURRAY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted with respect to the Bowlin and AMD matters. Defendants have submitted new evidence meeting their burden of production, and Plaintiffs have submitted no evidence raising an issue of fact as to whether there was a republication such that the statute of limitations had not run. The motion is granted as to the AT&T matter. Defendants submitted new evidence as to the lack of any impact on the judgment, and Plaintiffs made no substantive response to those arguments or evidence. The possibility of excessive fees or mishandled documents is not a basis to deny the motion in the absence of evidence that there would have been a more advantageous outcome. The motion is denied with respect to the homeowners' association action. A plaintiff's statements at deposition are an inadequate basis to prove the absence of any evidence to assert a claim. Just because Mrs. Yeager is not aware of claims or evidence, does not establish that Plaintiffs' counsel will not be able to produce evidence at trial. Defendants have not carried their burden of production. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Defendants De La Pena & Mcdonald, Llp, Charles Murray And Steven E. Mcdonald'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP, CHARLES MURRAY, AND STEVEN E. MCDONALD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION.

Appearances:
Claudia L. Stone of Nemecek & Cole (818.788.9500) for defendants and moving parties;
John D. Gibson and Edward Gordon of Gibson & Gibson (661.323.0588) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion is denied. Defendants have not carried their initial burden of production with respect to any of the issues on which they seek adjudication. As to the Yeager v. Yeager case, it is insufficient to present the appellate record and leave the Court to analyze how the appeal would have been resolved without analysis from the moving party in its brief or separate statement as to why the appeal could not have been successful had defendants not missed the deadline to appeal. Indeed, the record establishes that an argument was presented in support of the appeal. As to Bowlin, defendants' moving papers do not exclude all ways in which plaintiff might have prevailed. The Court will not consider arguments raised for the first time on reply as to this or the other issues. As to AT&T, the moving papers do not establish that the plaintiff could not show injury from failure to obtain an expert witness on damages or to seek an injunction. As to AMD, the moving papers do not establish that plaintiff had no claim on which it could have prevailed had it not had to dismiss the complaint once the statute of limitations had run. As to the HOA case, the mere fact that the underlying case is on-going is insufficient to prove that plaintiffs cannot show any injury. As to the unclean hands defense, the issue turns on all the competing factual circumstances and considerations, which must be weighed by the finder of fact. As to Victoria, she is a proper party as to the HOA case, and the Court cannot summarily adjudicate that claim for the reasons set out above. The Court notes that the moving parties recite background facts but provide virtually no legal analysis to support their prayer for summary judgment. The separate statement contains no facts pinning down the plaintiffs to any particular theories or eliminating the possibility that plaintiffs have some viable theory. The motion is unsupported by any viable analysis. The Court can properly reject the motion on that basis as well. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Connie J. Parchman, CSR #6137 (510.853.5260) (504)
